title: New ‘call a boomer’ phone line connects young people in Boston to seniors in Nevada
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Matter Neuroscience launched a 'call a boomer' phone line linking Boston youth with seniors in Reno, Nevada.
Phones are located near Boston University in Brookline and Sierra Manor, a senior facility in Reno, offering free calls.
Conversations are recorded for potential social media posts, aiming to bridge generational gaps and reduce loneliness.

title: Gas prices keep climbing, and Massachusetts may see some of the biggest hikes
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Gas prices in Massachusetts have risen sharply to an average of $3.40 per gallon due to disrupted global oil markets.
Diesel prices in Massachusetts increased from $4.04 to $4.83 per gallon in one week, affecting transportation costs.
New England's reliance on imported fuel and lack of local refineries make the region vulnerable to global oil market changes.

title: Patriots reportedly agree to 4-year contract with WR Romeo Doubs
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Patriots agree to a four-year contract with WR Romeo Doubs, worth up to $80 million.
Doubs joins Patriots from Packers after his best 2025 season: 55 catches, 724 yards, six touchdowns.
Doubs will support QB Drake Maye after the Patriots cut Stefon Diggs, aiming to boost passing game.

title: Airbnb is offering $750 to new hosts in World Cup cities, including Boston
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Airbnb offers $750 to new hosts in World Cup cities, including Boston and Providence, to meet high accommodation demand.
More than 380,000 soccer fans are expected to use Airbnb during the World Cup, increasing search averages by 80%.
Boston hosts projected to earn $5,200 on average, second highest after New York/New Jersey for World Cup accommodations.

title: Wellesley native running the Boston Marathon to honor family with Alzheimer’s
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Colin Rowan, 53, from Portland, Oregon, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Alzheimer's Association MA/NH Chapter.
Rowan's participation honors his mother and grandmother, both of whom have suffered from dementia-related conditions.
As a Massachusetts native, Rowan's motivation stems from childhood memories of the Boston Marathon in Wellesley.

title: Police are searching for North Shore suspect in string of indecent assaults
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Revere Police seek Hispanic male suspect in three indecent assault cases near Beachmont MBTA Station from January to March.
Incidents occurred at night, between 10 p.m. and 11 p.m., with suspect approaching female victims from behind.
Police urge residents to check surveillance footage and report any information or suspicious activity to authorities.

title: Wu announces millions in new funding for immigrant services
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston Mayor Michelle Wu announced $4.4 million in new funding to support immigrant services through a public-private partnership.
Funds will aid immigrants with legal services, basic necessities, mental health resources, and second-language learning, among other supports.
Boston Foundation, Barr Foundation, and United Way of Massachusetts Bay are key donors, contributing over $3.1 million.

title: Report: Flag football game featuring Tom Brady moved out of Saudi Arabia
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Fanatics Flag Football Classic featuring Tom Brady is moved from Riyadh to Los Angeles due to Middle East tensions.
The game will be held at BMO Stadium on March 21 and televised on Fox Sports, with Brady and Jalen Hurts as captains.
US Embassy in Riyadh was hit by Iranian drones; US and Israel are conducting military strikes against Iran.

title: Why this Boston Marathon qualifier is running for the American Heart Association
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Blake Smith, 28 from Church Hill, Tennessee, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the American Heart Association.
Smith was diagnosed with Idiopathic Hypertrophic Subaortic Stenosis at 14 but was later cleared to continue running.
Raising funds for the American Heart Association is personal for Smith as he appreciates running and his support network.

title: Cohasset man charged after allegedly shooting family dog while hunting coyotes from his window
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Christopher Lucas, 59, charged after allegedly shooting family dog while hunting coyotes from his second-floor window.
Lucas lacked a valid hunting license and acted outside legal hunting season, according to Cohasset Police.
Charges include animal cruelty and discharging a firearm near a dwelling; scheduled for arraignment in Quincy District Court.
